questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following describes a key procedural difference between the Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha regarding the consideration of Private Member Bills?

options.text: The Lok Sabha requires a two-month notice for introduction, while the Rajya Sabha requires only one month.
The Lok Sabha discusses these bills every Friday, whereas the Rajya Sabha discusses them only on every alternate Friday.
The Speaker of Lok Sabha can reject a bill's introduction, but the Chairman of Rajya Sabha has no such power.
Nominated members can introduce these bills in the Rajya Sabha but are prohibited from doing so in the Lok Sabha.

questions.explanation: According to the parliamentary mechanism, the Lok Sabha allots the last two-and-a-half hours of business every Friday for Private Member Bills. In contrast, the Rajya Sabha allots two and a half hours on every alternate Friday.
questions.takeaway: While both houses dedicate Friday afternoons to Private Member Bills, the frequency differs: every Friday in Lok Sabha vs. alternate Fridays in Rajya Sabha.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Lok Sabha allots last two-and-a-half hours of business every Friday for consideration and discussion on the Bill. Rajya Sabha allots two and a half hours (from 2.30 p.m. to 5.00 p.m.) on every alternate Friday.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: In the context of Indian Parliamentary procedure, what is the immediate effect of 'Adjournment Sine Die' on the business of the House?

options.text: All pending bills in the House immediately lapse and must be reintroduced.
The House is officially prorogued by the Presiding Officer.
Pending bills remain unaffected, but most notices for questions and motions lapse.
The House is dissolved, and fresh elections must be notified by the President.

questions.explanation: Adjournment Sine Die terminates a sitting without a fixed date for reconvening. It does not affect pending bills in that House, but most notices (questions, motions, resolutions) lapse and must be resubmitted in the next session.
questions.takeaway: Adjournment Sine Die ends a sitting indefinitely but preserves pending bills, unlike notices which typically lapse.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: All bills that are pending in the same House remain unaffected. Most notices (e.g., questions, motions, resolutions) lapse and must be resubmitted in the next session.
